_comment: FR-013 / SC-007 .NET code<->doc calibration gold set. Each pair is a C# member chunk and a documentation passage with a RELATIONSHIP label. 'related' = the doc passage is ABOUT this member (the band where doc-drift hides and the finder SHOULD surface a candidate); 'unrelated' = the doc passage is about a different API (the finder should NOT surface it). The drift/no-drift framing: a renamed method or a changed signature paired with a stale doc is still semantically 'related' (same intent/surface), which is exactly why the finder must flag it for a human/grep look. Documented in docs/calibration/009-dotnet-gold-set.md.

pairs.label: related
pairs.note: A method renamed Authenticate->SignIn; the XML-doc summary still says 'Authenticate'. Same behaviour, stale name -> drift the finder should surface.
pairs.doc: Call `Authenticate(username, password)` to sign a user in. It hashes the password and verifies it against the credential store, returning an AuthResult.

pairs.label: related
pairs.note: A method that gained a CancellationToken parameter; the README sentence describes the old two-arg form. Same operation, stale prose.
pairs.doc: Use DownloadReportAsync(source, destination) to fetch the report from the given URL and save it to a local file path.

pairs.label: related
pairs.note: A property that changed from a count to a boolean 'IsEmpty'; the doc still describes a numeric count.
pairs.code:
/// <summary>Whether the queue currently holds no items.</summary>
public bool IsEmpty => _items.Count == 0;
pairs.doc: The Count property returns the number of items currently waiting in the queue.

pairs.label: related
pairs.note: A method whose doc STILL matches (true negative for drift, but still a 'related' pair the finder correctly surfaces as a look-here).
pairs.code:
/// <summary>Add two integers and return their sum.</summary>
public int Add(int a, int b) => a + b;
pairs.doc: The Add method takes two integers and returns the sum of the two values.

pairs.label: related
pairs.note: A validation guard paired with a doc describing the same validation rule.
pairs.doc: Order totals are validated on entry: a negative total is rejected with an ArgumentOutOfRangeException before the order is persisted.

pairs.label: related
pairs.note: A retry helper paired with a doc that explains the retry/backoff policy.
pairs.doc: Transient failures are retried automatically with exponential backoff, doubling the delay each attempt until the configured maximum number of attempts is reached.

pairs.label: unrelated
pairs.note: An auth sign-in method paired with a doc passage about invoice billing — different surface entirely.
pairs.doc: Monthly invoices are generated on the first of each month. Each invoice aggregates the customer's metered usage and applies any active discount before the dunning cycle begins.

pairs.label: unrelated
pairs.note: A pure arithmetic method paired with a doc about TLS handshake configuration.
pairs.code: public int Add(int a, int b) => a + b;
pairs.doc: Configure the minimum TLS version and the cipher suite ordering in the transport options so the handshake negotiates a modern, forward-secret connection.

pairs.label: unrelated
pairs.note: A queue property paired with a doc about structured logging sinks.
pairs.doc: Structured log events are written to the configured sinks with a correlation id, the event level, and a rendered message template for downstream aggregation.

pairs.label: unrelated
pairs.note: A validation guard paired with a doc about cache eviction.
pairs.doc: The in-memory cache evicts the least-recently-used entries once the configured size budget is exceeded, keeping the hot working set resident.
